tips: Use multi-stage builds to keep final images small
Order Dockerfile layers from least to most frequently changing for caching
Use .dockerignore to exclude node_modules and other unnecessary files
Prefer specific tags over 'latest' for reproducible builds
Use docker compose for local development with multiple services
Health checks let Docker know when your container is actually ready
Use --init flag to handle zombie processes in containers
Set resource limits with --memory and --cpus to prevent noisy neighbors
Use volumes for persistent data and bind mounts for development
Keep each container focused on a single concern
Use ARG for build-time variables and ENV for runtime configuration
Scan images with 'docker scout' for known vulnerabilities
